llcFilesWith: North Dakota Secretary of State (Trade Name Registration through the FirstStop portal)
soleProprietorFilesWith: North Dakota Secretary of State (the same statewide trade name registration applies to individuals and sole proprietors)
feeBasis: The Secretary of State fee to register a trade name through the FirstStop portal is $25.
renewalRule: A trade name registration is effective for five years and must be renewed through the FirstStop portal to stay in force.
protectionNote: North Dakota trade name registration grants the owner the exclusive right to the name in North Dakota: once a trade name is registered, no other business may file a name with the Secretary of State that is the same as, or deceptively similar to, it (N.D.C.C. ch. 47-25).
searchNote: Registered trade names appear in the FirstStop business search alongside entity names.
notes: North Dakota registers trade names statewide with the Secretary of State under N.D.C.C. ch. 47-25, through the FirstStop portal ($25, five-year term). Unlike most states' DBAs, a North Dakota trade name grants an exclusive right: no other business may register a name that is the same as or deceptively similar to a registered trade name, and the trade name is part of the entity-name distinguishability screen. Both LLCs and sole proprietors use it, and there is no publication requirement.

sources.title: Trade Names — Registration and exclusive right
